uPWarrior: focus on your goal: build territory
C[uPWarrior: focus on your goal: build territory
C[uPWarrior: how much territory to you need to win?
HanaUchima: Yeah sorry I got a drink, and I've never read anything thats said you needed a certain ammount
uPWarrior: but your territory needs to be more than mine!
uPWarrior: you need +- half the board
uPWarrior: of course it won't be clear cut, where will be ""pockets"" of territory
uPWarrior: especially as the board gets larger
uPWarrior: but if you look at the board right now
uPWarrior: what is going to be your territory?
uPWarrior: what are you fighting for?
uPWarrior: and what is going to be my territory? (which part will you let me take? you can't take everything!)
HanaUchima: Right now? I'm going for the right side of the board
uPWarrior: how much territory did you get from F9?
uPWarrior: I'm not looking for a number
uPWarrior: just the feeling: wasn't there something better?
uPWarrior: like.. E8? it would still be bad, but it is next to F9
uPWarrior: and intuitively it gives more territory, right?
uPWarrior: because G3 is in danger
uPWarrior: and you don't get the right side anymore
uPWarrior: you need to decide if you want them dead or alive
uPWarrior: you know what it takes to be alive, right? 2 eyes?
HanaUchima: Where am I from? How is that relevant
uPWarrior: I wanted to know if you were a native english speaker
uPWarrior: because you are not writing much
HanaUchima: I am, I just don't have much to say XD
uPWarrior: it gets hard to point you in the right direction if I don't know what you are thinking
uPWarrior: I didn't get if you knew what 2 eyes are
HanaUchima: I know what 2 eyes are I just don't get how to use it in this scenario
uPWarrior: if you want G3 to live, you must think ""do I have enough space to create 2 eyes there, or do I need to connect it to something bigger?""
uPWarrior: the same goes for C3
uPWarrior: in general, that's a good question to ask to see if you need to help a stone
uPWarrior: if your opponent plays next to it
uPWarrior: you first ask 1. do I need that stone, or can I let it die and get something nice in return?
uPWarrior: and 2. can it live if I ignore what my opponent played? does it have enough space for 2 eyes? do I need to connect it somewhere?
uPWarrior: the space around G3 seems tiny for it to live alone
uPWarrior: so you may want to sacrifice it
uPWarrior: try to make C3 live instead
uPWarrior: try to make 2 eyes there
uPWarrior: (it has more breathing room than G3!)
HanaUchima: I get that but in all the things Ive seen showing what two eyes look like it doesn't show how you can make two eyes in this case because right now it doesn't make sense to me
uPWarrior: assuming you can't connect C3 anywhere (it seems tough)
uPWarrior: you need to surround enough space
uPWarrior: so that you can create 2 eyes inside
uPWarrior: a few ideas would be
uPWarrior: expanding the region between C3 and the walls
uPWarrior: so that you can create the needed shape
C[uPWarrior: if I want to kill you, I will do the opposite
uPWarrior: I will try to reduce your eye shape
uPWarrior: I already know this shape, I've seen it a lot before
uPWarrior: I know it can be killed
uPWarrior: but I won't try very hard
uPWarrior: I want to see if you can survive in a mild condition
C[uPWarrior: let me know if you are lost before you make a move
uPWarrior: ok, so you need breathing room, space
uPWarrior: there are a few options here actually
uPWarrior: you are telling me ""look, if you don't block at B6, I'm going to crawl all the way to the top edge and I'll find 2 eyes somewhere on the left side""
uPWarrior: so I'm pretty much forced to block
uPWarrior: and you can play on the bottom next
uPWarrior: or you can start from the bottom
uPWarrior: not focusing on any particular move: you are trying to enlarge the are in the corner
HanaUchima: Yeah that sorta makes sense, so in this case you're saying I could either move to B5 to continue with the eye thing or D3 or E2 and enlarge the area in the corner
uPWarrior: enlarging the area is also for the eye thing
uPWarrior: eyes are delimited regions in the board where the opponent can't play because he will be killed
uPWarrior: you need space for that
uPWarrior: B5 will give extra space on the left
uPWarrior: the others will give space on the bottom
uPWarrior: starting from the bottom is probably more important here, because there is more space there
uPWarrior: but I don't think you should worry about that for now
uPWarrior: asking the right questions: 1. you need to live
uPWarrior: try to get as much space as you can
uPWarrior: I might not have given you enough time on the clock
uPWarrior: there is no way to give extra time in the server right now
HanaUchima: So what do we do?
uPWarrior: but I think there is a limit
uPWarrior: the result is not really important
uPWarrior: now I want to block
C[uPWarrior: so that you don't connect E2 to G3 somehow
uPWarrior: E2 is a good move in this case, because G3 wasn't 100% dead
uPWarrior: it's what we call ""exploiting aji"" - ""aji"" is the japanese word for the bad things an opponent can make
uPWarrior: with the shape of the stones that was left
uPWarrior: I played next to E2
uPWarrior: let me give a smaller variation
uPWarrior: (the shape between C3 and E2 can be cut)
uPWarrior: it is not very solid
uPWarrior: to you need to connect them better if you want E2 to live
uPWarrior: D3 and D2 are obvious
HanaUchima: Actually now that I think of it D3 would give me a bigger area for the corner but I feel like D2 makes it easier to corner off that area
uPWarrior: based on what I've told you, D3 does make a bigger region
uPWarrior: I can force you play D2 by playing E3
uPWarrior: can you see what happens if you don't play D2?
HanaUchima: All I can see is you get more space
uPWarrior: in the last variation I showed
uPWarrior: E2 is in immediate danger
HanaUchima: Yeah I see it now
uPWarrior: (I'm asking you to read slightly ahead, but the diagrams help)
uPWarrior: but sooner or later black will have to play D2
uPWarrior: because C3 is not connected to E2 with D2 alone, right?
uPWarrior: I'm sorry if this game is being more wordy than you would like
uPWarrior: you are not giving me much feedback so I don't know when to stop
HanaUchima: Well this is helping me a lot actually so I dont mind
uPWarrior: lets try to finish this corner faster
HanaUchima: So I can put down D2 now?
C[uPWarrior: the shape is getting clearer, white will try to get the top left and the bottom right, black the other 2 corners
uPWarrior: I guess black needs to block white if he wants space to make 2 eyes, so go ahead
uPWarrior: by blocking I mean B4, I hope it was clear
C[uPWarrior: I want a big corner for myself
uPWarrior: remember what we said about D2 helping E2 because of E3?
uPWarrior: you can do something similar
uPWarrior: and force B6 (or you can capture B5)
uPWarrior: can you see this 2 or 3 moves ahead?
HanaUchima: Not really no, I can't see how it'd help me later
uPWarrior: it doesn't help much to be honest
uPWarrior: I was just creating a parallel between what we talked about before and this side
uPWarrior: you don't lose anything anyway
uPWarrior: you were just forcing me to play inside my ""future territory"", B6
uPWarrior: so it would still be 1 point to you, nothing great
uPWarrior: but if you want something greater, something that gives territory,,
HanaUchima: Yeah that makes more sense at least
uPWarrior: there are many option that start fights, but I want to keep it peaceful
HanaUchima: So I can go for H4 or do you want me to stick with going for B6
C[uPWarrior: as long as things click in your mind, all is good
uPWarrior: playing with a purpose, having a plan
uPWarrior: that's the hard part in the begging
uPWarrior: I can't separate H4 from H6
uPWarrior: do you see what happens if white plays H5? what do you do then?
HanaUchima: Couldn't I just play G5 or J5
uPWarrior: so I can't attack H4 directly
uPWarrior: if I don't do anything
uPWarrior: you will just connect H4 to G3
uPWarrior: and it will live again (damn aji)
C[uPWarrior: making sure it really dies
uPWarrior: I played close to H4
HanaUchima: The white piece? Yes....I think, because couldn't I just do this
uPWarrior: white won't run to J3
uPWarrior: white will capture G3 at G2, right?
HanaUchima: Oh yeah I didn't see that
uPWarrior: is it in danger, if you don't protect it?
uPWarrior: the black stone you played
HanaUchima: I dunno because if I played G2 I feel like eventually after a couple moves there'd just be several pieces surrounded
HanaUchima: So instead wouldn't I just go for H5
uPWarrior: ideas so H4 doesn't die
uPWarrior: there is a better plan
uPWarrior: something that also defends H4, but forces me to respond
uPWarrior: so then you have the initiative to play somewhere else
uPWarrior: this is what you plan
HanaUchima: Oh that makes sense. Because if white tried to go to H5 it'd be dead
uPWarrior: is it ok if we end here?
uPWarrior: the rest would be boring
uPWarrior: the regions are +- well defined
uPWarrior: we are trying to make our corners as big as possible
uPWarrior: I don't know who is ahead right now, the corners look to be the same size
uPWarrior: yes, it is very close as is
uPWarrior: I hope you see the goal
HanaUchima: Yeah it makes a lot more sense
uPWarrior: it makes you scared because you have no idea what you are doing
uPWarrior: but once you grasp the basics, it is very fun
HanaUchima: Yeah :D Ill let you know how I do later, bye!
